_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the docx Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript (directly observed tool_use, name=Skill, skill=docx). SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration, not the primary gate.
_grading.fire_signature: Session reaches for python-docx (install check, then Document()/built-in style names); an existing .docx is opened and modified run-by-run, never rebuilt from plain text; delivery includes a reopen-and-assert verification.
_grading.silent_signature: No python-docx ceremony: prose answered as prose/Markdown, slides routed to pptx, spreadsheets to xlsx, PDF reading to pdf. Faking Word output (Markdown renamed .docx, bold+size pseudo-headings) counts as a failure even if the skill fired.
_grading.surface: Claude Code headless (claude -p), project-scope install in this repo
_grading.surface_amended: 2026-07-13: aligned personal->project scope per architecture-contract Decision 8 (capability skills are project-scoped); amended pre-first-run, no runs had occurred.
_grading.gate: >=3/4 should-fire prompts fire; >=2/3 near-miss prompts stay silent
_grading.gate_note: 3/4-fire and 2/3-silent are the small-N analogues of the governors' >=5/6 / >=3/4 convention (rate thresholds per architecture-contract invariant-3 scope note); measured rates get recorded as rates.

evals.prompt: Write up these onboarding steps as a Word doc I can send to the new hires — needs a title, section headings, and a checklist.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: docx loads; document built with python-docx using built-in styles (Title, Heading 1/2, List Bullet), saved as .docx, reopened to verify — not Markdown renamed or bold+size fake headings.

evals.prompt: Create a .docx summary of the Q2 results with a table of the key metrics.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: docx loads; real .docx with a styled table and a bold header row, verified by reopening.

evals.prompt: Fill in this Word template offer_letter.docx with the candidate name and start date — the formatting has to stay exactly as HR set it.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: docx loads; placeholders replaced run-by-run (never paragraph.text rebuilds) so bold/styled fields survive; output reopened to confirm formatting.

evals.prompt: Edit report.docx to change the Q2 revenue figure from 1.2M to 1.5M without breaking the formatting.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: docx loads; document opened in place, target run's text modified, surrounding run/style structure preserved, saved and re-verified.

evals.prompt: Turn these quarterly numbers into a spreadsheet with a totals row and a summary tab.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: tabular data with computation belongs to the xlsx sibling; no python-docx, no .docx produced.

evals.prompt: What does contract.pdf say about the termination clause? Pull out the relevant section.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: reading/extracting from an existing PDF belongs to the pdf sibling (parser output, not recall); docx stays out entirely.

evals.prompt: Write up the meeting notes from today's standup so I can paste them into Slack.
evals.expected_output: SILENT: output lives as plain text/Markdown in chat — no skill needed; producing a .docx here is pure ceremony.
